By winkrun, July 12, 2026 · AI Engineering
Turn great design judgment into rules AI can execute directly.
Emil Kowalski, former designer at Vercel and Linear and now founder of animations.dev, has packaged Apple-level design taste into a set of executable AI “skills”.
Installation is as simple as running this single command:
npx skills@latest add emilkowalski/skillsOnce installed, your AI tools can follow these rules to generate interfaces or audit your existing code.
The repository currently includes five separate skills, and the standout addition is /apple-design. Kowalski went through his favorite WWDC design talks, distilled 17 core design and animation principles, and translated them into actionable rules that web developers can use directly with AI.
Why does this tool exist? In Kowalski’s own words:
“Agents don’t have great taste.”
AI-generated interfaces almost always feel just slightly off. An entrance animation uses ease-in when it should use ease-out. A solid border is used where a semi-transparent shadow would work better. Each issue is tiny on its own, but piled together they create that uncanny "something's wrong but I can't put my finger on it" feel that makes designs look unpolished.
This set of skills turns those vague, unspoken issues into clear, concrete rules and tells AI exactly how to fix them.
The five full skills included in the package are:

• emil-design-eng — The core parent skill, covers general animation and design guidance

• review-animations — Strict animation auditing following Kowalski’s personal design rules

• improve-animations — Scans an entire codebase for animation issues, outputs a prioritized improvement plan

• animation-vocabulary — Teaches AI to use the correct industry terminology when describing motion design

• apple-design — Apple design principles distilled from WWDC, adapted for web development
When asked why he chose the 2018 Designing Fluid Interfaces talk over newer resources like Apple’s Liquid Glass design system, Kowalski explained that the 2018 session remains one of the best design talks Apple has ever given, and its core principles are completely timeless.
After testing this tool out myself, I was genuinely impressed. It can analyze your current design gaps and deliver a clear improvement plan, helping you align with top-tier Apple design standards in just a few prompts.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Above is an example of the improved output after applying these rules.
This approach is brilliant: it distills reference knowledge into a skill AI can execute directly, instead of requiring a human to interpret and rephrase the knowledge for AI. Cutting out that middleman makes the whole process far more efficient and eliminates information loss. Encoding domain expertise directly into a format AI can consume might just become the dominant model for AI-assisted work going forward.
Kowalski says he’s gone fully “AI-pilled” lately, but not because he thinks AI will replace experts. Quite the opposite: AI doesn’t replace specialized skill — it amplifies it.
Check out the project on GitHub: https://github.com/emilkowalski/skills
